values.title: Ember & Bloom Homepage
values.hero_description: A wood-fired kitchen celebrating the seasons. Every dish begins with the finest local ingredients, kissed by flame and crafted with care. Join us for an unforgettable dining experience.
values.cta_description: Whether it is a romantic dinner for two, a family celebration, or a corporate gathering, we will make your evening exceptional. Book online or call us directly.

values.title: Wood-Fired King Salmon
values.body: Wild King salmon roasted over cherry wood, served with charred broccolini, fingerling potatoes, and a Meyer lemon herb butter. The wood fire lends a subtle smokiness that complements the rich, silky fish.

values.title: Dry-Aged Ribeye
values.body: 45-day dry-aged prime ribeye, seared over live oak and finished with bone marrow compound butter. Served with roasted garlic mashed potatoes and seasonal vegetables from our partner farm.

image.alt: Dry-aged ribeye steak on a plate
image.title: Dry-Aged Ribeye

values.title: Heirloom Tomato Salad
values.body: Peak-season heirloom tomatoes from Sunrise Farm with burrata, basil oil, aged balsamic, and Maldon sea salt. Simple, perfect, and available only when tomatoes are at their best.

values.title: Wild Mushroom Flatbread
values.body: Wood-fired flatbread topped with a mix of foraged wild mushrooms, truffle cream, fontina cheese, and fresh thyme. Our dough is naturally leavened for 72 hours before baking in the 800-degree oven.

values.title: Apple Galette
values.body: Rustic apple galette made with Honeycrisp apples from Orchard Hill Farm, brown butter, and a hint of cardamom. Served warm with house-made vanilla bean ice cream and salted caramel.

image.alt: Rustic apple galette with ice cream
image.title: Apple Galette

values.title: Smoked Old Fashioned
values.body: Our signature cocktail: bourbon smoked tableside with applewood chips, stirred with demerara syrup, aromatic bitters, and a flamed orange peel. A theatrical start to any evening.

values.title: Farm-to-Table Harvest Dinner
values.body:
Join us for a special five-course harvest dinner showcasing the best of the fall season. Chef Elena will prepare a prix fixe menu featuring ingredients sourced exclusively from our partner farms within 25 miles of the restaurant.

Wine pairings selected by our sommelier are available. This intimate dinner is limited to 40 guests.

values.title: Winemaker's Dinner with Willamette Valley Vineyards
values.body:
An exclusive evening pairing Chef Elena's wood-fired cuisine with wines from Willamette Valley Vineyards. Winemaker Sarah Kline will guide guests through each pairing, sharing stories of the harvest and the art of winemaking.

Five courses, five wines, one unforgettable evening. Space is limited to 30 guests.

image.alt: Wine glasses at a dinner table
image.title: Winemaker's Dinner

values.title: Sunday Morning Cooking Class
values.body:
Learn the fundamentals of wood-fired cooking in this hands-on class. Chef Elena will teach you how to manage heat, develop flavor through fire, and prepare three dishes in our wood-fired oven.

Class includes all ingredients, recipes to take home, and a glass of prosecco. Limited to 12 participants per session.

values.title: Unforgettable Anniversary Dinner
values.body: We celebrated our anniversary at Ember and Bloom and it exceeded every expectation. The wood-fired salmon was the best fish I have ever had. The smoked Old Fashioned was a showstopper. The staff made us feel truly special.

values.title: The Best Steak in Town
values.body: The dry-aged ribeye is absolutely phenomenal. The wood fire gives it a depth of flavor you simply cannot get at home. The bone marrow butter is pure indulgence. We have been back three times already this year.

values.title: Outstanding Vegetarian Options
values.body: As a vegetarian, I often feel like an afterthought at restaurants. Not here. The wild mushroom flatbread and the heirloom tomato salad were both extraordinary. The staff was knowledgeable about every ingredient and happy to customize dishes.

values.title: Perfect Sunday Brunch
values.body: The weekend brunch at Ember and Bloom is a hidden gem. Wood-fired eggs with chorizo, fresh-baked pastries, and the best coffee in the city. The garden patio is lovely on a sunny morning. Highly recommend making a reservation.

values.title: About Ember & Bloom
values.body:
Ember & Bloom opened in 2018 with a simple mission: to celebrate the flavors of the Pacific Northwest through the primal art of wood-fired cooking. Every dish that leaves our kitchen has been touched by flame.

Our Chef

Chef Elena Vasquez trained in the wood-fired kitchens of Spain and Argentina before returning home to open Ember & Bloom. Her cooking philosophy is rooted in restraint - letting exceptional ingredients speak for themselves with the enhancement of live fire.

Our Ingredients

We partner with over 15 local farms and purveyors within a 50-mile radius. Our menu changes weekly based on what is freshest and most flavorful. We believe great cooking starts long before the kitchen - it starts in the soil.

Our Kitchen

At the heart of the restaurant is a custom-built wood-burning oven and open hearth, fueled by sustainably harvested hardwoods. The open kitchen allows guests to watch the dance of fire and food.

values.title: Contact & Reservations
values.body:
We would love to welcome you to Ember & Bloom. Reservations are recommended, especially on weekends.

Hours

Dinner: Tuesday - Saturday, 5:00 PM - 10:00 PM
Brunch: Saturday & Sunday, 10:00 AM - 2:00 PM
Happy Hour: Tuesday - Friday, 4:00 PM - 6:00 PM

Location

Ember & Bloom
315 NW Everett Street
Portland, OR 97209

Contact

Reservations: (555) 890-1234
Events & Private Dining: events@emberandbloom.com
General: info@emberandbloom.com
